compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Why
Epic AC1 requires a **write-time guard that rejects private-key material in an identity
event**. The delivered `identity` create path (ddbe / change 558) passes `keys` straight
onto the CREATE event with no check, so a user could accidentally commit an SSH PRIVATE
key into the always-readable, replicated tickets store — a real (if narrow) footgun. The
epic completion-verifier flagged this as unmet. (Remediation of epic gnu-whale-ichor to
full AC coverage; see session_log fanatical-gentling-bushbaby.)

## What
Add a shared guard `reject_private_key_material(values: list[str])` in
`src/rebar/_commands/identity.py` that raises `CommandError` if any value contains a
private-key PEM/OpenSSH header — matching (case-insensitive) any of:
`-----BEGIN OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY-----`, `-----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----`,
`-----BEGIN EC PRIVATE KEY-----`, `-----BEGIN DSA PRIVATE KEY-----`,
`-----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----`, `-----BEGIN ENCRYPTED PRIVATE KEY-----` (i.e. the generic
`BEGIN … PRIVATE KEY` family). Call it:
- in `create_identity_core` on the `keys` list BEFORE `create_core` appends the CREATE
  event (no event written on rejection);
- in `add_identity_key` on the `public_key` BEFORE the KEY_ADD event is appended.
A legitimate authorized-keys public line (`ssh-ed25519 AAAA…`) passes unchanged.

## Scope
In: the `reject_private_key_material` guard + its two call sites (identity CREATE keys[]
and KEY_ADD public_key). Out: any other behavior. The guard function lives in the ddbe
commit (558); the `add_identity_key` call site lives in the e165 commit (563) — both are
amended (the guard fn is defined in 558, so 563 can call it).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `create_identity(name, email, keys=[<a string containing "BEGIN OPENSSH PRIVATE
  KEY">])` raises (CommandError/RebarError) and appends NO event (store unchanged); a test
  asserts the raise and that no identity was created.
- [ ] `add_identity_key(identity, <a private-key string>)` raises and appends no KEY_ADD
  event (test).
- [ ] A normal authorized-keys public line (`ssh-ed25519 AAAA…`) is accepted by both
  `create_identity(keys=[…])` and `add_identity_key` (test).
- [ ] The guard matches the common private-key header family case-insensitively
  (parametrized test over the RSA/EC/DSA/OPENSSH/PKCS8/ENCRYPTED headers).

## file_impact
- `src/rebar/_commands/identity.py` (reject_private_key_material guard + create/key-add call sites)
compiled_state.title: identity: reject private-key material at the identity write-gate (epic AC1)
